claim: UCB T cells maintain high TCR diversity after transplantation.

result.claim: UCB T cells maintain high TCR diversity after transplantation.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from paper 27910499, "Quantitative assessment of T-cell repertoire recovery after hematopoietic stem cell transplantation." Proposition 234 explicitly states, "After 6 months, cord blood-graft recipients approximated the TCR diversity of healthy individuals." The abstract of this paper further clarifies that "cord blood-graft recipients approximated the TCR diversity of healthy individuals, whereas recipients of T cell-depleted peripheral-blood stem cell grafts had 28-fold and 14-fold lower CD4(+) and CD8(+) T cell diversities, respectively." This direct comparison highlights that UCB T cells maintain high TCR diversity after transplantation, comparable to healthy individuals, unlike other graft types. While the paper's citation count is 0 and author/year information is unavailable, the directness and specificity of the findings provide strong support for the claim.

evidence.text: In the example with the transplant, donor cells contributing to the developing testis retained a female identity.
evidence.source: Somatic sex identity is cell-autonomous in the chicken

evidence.text: Delayed T cell recovery and restricted T cell receptor diversity after allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation are associated with increased risks of infection and cancer relapse.
evidence.source: Quantitative assessment of T-cell repertoire recovery after hematopoietic stem cell transplantation

evidence.text: After 12 months, the deficiencies in CD8(+) T cell diversity had not improved.
evidence.source: Quantitative assessment of T-cell repertoire recovery after hematopoietic stem cell transplantation

evidence.text: Mouse studies indicate that tissue localization of T cell subsets is important for both protective immunity and immunoregulation.

evidence.text: Host macrophages retained the capacity to expand after bone marrow transplantation.
evidence.source: Tissue-resident macrophages self-maintain locally throughout adult life with minimal contribution from circulating monocytes.

evidence.text: Effector memory T cells (T(EM)) were found only in the lungs and small intestine of pediatric subjects.

evidence.text: Regulatory T (T(reg)) cells comprise 30-40% of CD4(+) T cells in pediatric tissues.

evidence.text: In adult tissues the main subset of T cells are memory T cells.

evidence.text: T cell receptor (TCR-CD3) triggering involves both receptor clustering and conformational changes at the cytoplasmic tails of the CD3 subunits.
evidence.source: Full activation of the T cell receptor requires both clustering and conformational changes at CD3.

evidence.text: Memory CD4+ T cells enhance production of multiple innate inflammatory cytokines and chemokines (IICs) in the lung.
evidence.source: Memory CD4+ T cells induce innate responses independently of pathogen

evidence.text: T cell activation is predicated on the interaction between the T cell receptor and peptide-major histocompatibility ligands.
evidence.source: The stimulatory potency of T cell antigens is influenced by the formation of the immunological synapse.

evidence.text: The conformational change at CD3 is required for T cell activation.
evidence.source: Full activation of the T cell receptor requires both clustering and conformational changes at CD3.
